description: Verify `claude mcp add` actually registered the server before declaring success.
rationale: `claude mcp add` exits 0 even when registration fails due to read-only `~/.claude/` or old Claude Code versions (F4). This patch immediately detects the failure with an actionable diagnostic, eliminating the bounce where users finish install, try `search_memory`, get "tool not found," and have no idea which layer failed.
expected_impact: Users with read-only `~/.claude/` or outdated Claude Code (enterprise setups, locked-down VMs) currently finish install successfully then fail silently at first MCP call; this patch moves the failure to install-time with a one-line fix suggestion, shortening time-to-working-memory by eliminating one full retry cycle.
unified_diff:
--- a/installer/setup.sh
+++ b/installer/setup.sh
@@ -94,7 +94,14 @@
 claude mcp add -s user --transport http \
   cctx-memory "$CCTX_URL/mcp/" \
   --header "Authorization: Bearer $CCTX_TOKEN"
 
+# Verify MCP server was actually registered (claude mcp add exits 0 even on failure)
+if ! claude mcp list 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^cctx-memory:'; then
+  echo "  ERROR: MCP server registration failed."
+  echo "  Check: claude mcp list — if empty, ~/.claude/ may be read-only or Claude Code outdated."
+  exit 1
+fi
+
 echo "  Added MCP server: cctx-memory (user scope)"
 
 # --- Step 4: Install hook scripts ---

score_reasons.time_to_value: Detects F4 misconfig at install-time with actionable diagnostic, eliminating one full retry cycle for users with read-only dirs or old Claude Code.
score_reasons.failure_mode_coverage: Cites F4 and handles primary triggers (read-only directories, outdated Claude Code) but may miss project-scope conflict edge case.
score_reasons.minimalism: Net +7 lines, no new dependencies, reuses existing patterns (grep, echo format), single focused check.
score_reasons.taste: Style matches exactly, description is one precise sentence, user-facing output gives exact next action, zero marketing language.

scope_violation_reason: No violation — modifies installer/setup.sh which is in the allowed paths list.
